toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run `dotnet test` and collect a crash dump if any test crashes the host. This is a .NET 9 MSTest project using the Microsoft Testing Platform (`TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` is set to true).

For collecting crash dumps on test host crashes, the recommended approach is to use the `--crashdump` option (available through Microsoft Testing Platform) or set the `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump` environment variable.

Since the project uses `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`, I can use the `--crashdump` command-line option which is provided by the Microsoft Testing Platform's crash dump extension. 

I'll go with the `--crashdump` option and run the tests with it enabled.

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-4fd7f9aec8cc41ebbebd9e346ffdbe2f && dotnet test --crashdump 2>&1", "description": "Run dotnet test with crash dump collection", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.content: The `--crashdump` flag needs to be passed after `--` so it reaches the test platform runner, not MSBuild:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crashdump via -- separator

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crashdump via -- separator

data.reasoningText:
The `--crashdump` switch isn't being passed through correctly. Since `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` is true, I need to pass it differently. Let me try using `--` to pass arguments directly to the test platform, or use the environment variable approach.

Actually, with the Microsoft Testing Platform integration in `dotnet test`, the way to pass platform-specific options is via `--`. Let me try:

```
dotnet test -- --crashdump
```

Wait, actually looking at the error more carefully - the `--crashdump` is being treated as an MSBuild switch. With the newer `dotnet test` integration for Microsoft Testing Platform, options after `--` are passed to the test runner. 

Let me test that approach.

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. Here's the command:

```
dotnet test -- --crashdump
```

The `--` separator passes `--crashdump` to the Microsoft Testing Platform runner (enabled by `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` in your `.csproj`). If a test crashes the host process, a crash dump (`.dmp` file) will be collected in the test results directory for post-mortem analysis.
